slides.title: Digital products that put your business to work
slides.text: Devline is a Georgian software studio — we build web and mobile applications, DevOps pipelines, and infrastructure with a full-cycle approach: from idea to production.

slides.title: From idea to production — one team
slides.text: We build web and mobile applications with a single team, so design, development, and launch fit together naturally.

slides.title: Your infrastructure — our responsibility
slides.text: CI/CD pipelines, cloud and on-prem environments, monitoring, and 24/7 support — releases without the stress.

section.text: Four disciplines under one roof — from development to marketing, all working in sync.

section.title: Engineers who know production
section.text: Devline.digital brings together a team with real experience working in enterprise environments — development, DevOps, systems administration, and security. To us, a good product isn't just a pretty interface: it's a stable pipeline, measurable performance, and a team that always responds.

section.title: A website that puts your business to work

We set up and manage Jira and Jira Service Management — from workflows to Customer Portal and SLAs, tailored to your team's real process.

seo.description: Developer, JSM & Jira, design, and marketing — a full-cycle digital studio in Georgia.

jira.q: Do you set up Jira from scratch, or fix an existing project?
jira.a: Both. We can configure new Jira projects from zero, or audit and restructure an existing setup that's become slow or confusing to use.

jira.q: Can you customize workflows for our specific process?
jira.a: Yes — we build custom statuses, transitions, and automation rules that match how your team actually works, whether that's Scrum, Kanban, or a hybrid.

jira.q: Do you set up reporting and dashboards?
jira.a: Yes. We configure dashboards and reports (velocity, burndown, cycle time) so leads and stakeholders see progress without asking for status updates.

jira.q: Can Jira integrate with our other tools?
jira.a: Yes — we connect Jira with Slack, Bitbucket/GitHub, Confluence, and other tools so updates and notifications flow automatically.
We include onboarding sessions and documentation as part of every setup, so the team adopts the new structure quickly and consistently.

slack.q: Can you clean up a messy Slack workspace?
slack.a: Yes — we audit existing channels, archive what's unused, and restructure the workspace around teams and projects so it's easy to navigate again.

slack.q: Can you build custom Slack bots?
slack.a: Yes. We build bots and workflows for common requests — standup reminders, ticket updates, approval flows — using Slack's native Workflow Builder or custom integrations.

slack.q: Can Slack notify us automatically from Jira or ClickUp?
slack.a: Yes — we set up two-way integrations so status changes, comments, and mentions in Jira/ClickUp post directly into the right Slack channel.

slack.q: How do you prevent notification overload?
slack.a: We configure notification rules and channel defaults so people only get pinged for what's actually relevant to them — less noise, faster response.

slack.q: How long does a Slack setup take?
slack.a: A typical workspace restructuring and integration setup takes about 1 week, depending on team size and number of tools connected.

figma.q: Do you design from scratch, or work with an existing brand?
figma.a: Both. We can create a new visual direction from scratch, or design within your existing brand guidelines and design system.

figma.q: What do I receive at the end — just images, or something developers can use?
figma.a: Developers get a fully organized Figma file with components, auto-layout, and Dev Mode inspection — so they can pull exact spacing, colors, and assets directly.

figma.q: Can you build an interactive prototype I can test with users?
figma.a: Yes — we build clickable prototypes that simulate real navigation and interactions, so you can test the flow before a single line of code is written.

figma.q: Do you design for both web and mobile?
figma.a: Yes — we design responsive web interfaces and native-feeling mobile app screens, using the same design system for consistency.

figma.q: How long does a design project take?
figma.a: A typical project — wireframes through final UI — takes 2–5 weeks depending on the number of screens and rounds of feedback.

photoshop.q: What kind of images do you edit?
photoshop.a: Product photos, portraits, marketing banners, social media graphics, and composite images — anything that needs retouching, color correction, or manipulation.

photoshop.q: Can you make our social media graphics consistent with our brand?
photoshop.a: Yes — we work from your brand colors, fonts, and style guide (or help create one) so every graphic looks like it belongs to the same brand.

photoshop.q: Do you do photo compositing — combining multiple images?
photoshop.a: Yes — from simple background swaps to complex photorealistic composites blending several source images into one seamless result.
Our standard process includes revision rounds until you're happy with the result — we typically finalize within 2–3 rounds of feedback.

photoshop.q: How fast can you turn around a batch of images?
photoshop.a: A standard batch (10–20 images) is usually delivered within 3–5 business days, depending on complexity.

illustrator.q: What do I get with a logo design project?
illustrator.a: A complete brand mark with variations (horizontal, icon-only, monochrome), delivered as vector files (SVG, AI, EPS) plus a brand book covering colors, fonts, and usage rules.

illustrator.q: Can you design a custom icon set for our product or app?
illustrator.a: Yes — we design cohesive icon sets that match your brand style, delivered as scalable vector assets ready to drop into your app or website.

illustrator.q: Do you prepare files for print, not just digital use?
illustrator.a: Yes — we prepare print-ready files with correct color modes (CMYK), bleed, and resolution for business cards, packaging, banners, and other printed materials.

illustrator.q: Will the designs stay sharp at any size?
illustrator.a: Yes — everything is built in vector format, so logos and icons scale from a favicon to a billboard without losing quality.

illustrator.q: How long does a logo or icon set take?
illustrator.a: A logo project typically takes 1–2 weeks including revisions; a full icon set usually takes about the same, depending on the number of icons.

premiere.q: What types of videos do you edit?
premiere.a: Promotional videos, product demos, social media clips, testimonials, and motivational/brand content — from raw footage to a polished final cut.

premiere.q: Do you handle color correction and audio cleanup?
premiere.a: Yes — every edit includes color grading for a consistent look and audio processing (noise reduction, leveling) so the final video sounds as good as it looks.

premiere.q: Can you add subtitles or captions?
premiere.a: Yes — we add accurate subtitles in your language(s) of choice, formatted for accessibility and for platforms where videos autoplay muted.

premiere.q: Do you export in the right format for each platform?
premiere.a: Yes — we deliver versions optimized for each destination (Instagram Reels, YouTube, LinkedIn, etc.), matching the right aspect ratio and specs.

premiere.q: How fast can you turn around an edit?
premiere.a: A standard short-form video (under 2 minutes) is usually delivered in 3–5 business days; longer or more complex edits take a bit more.

metaMarketing.q: Do you handle both the strategy and the ad creative?
metaMarketing.a: Yes — we plan the campaign strategy and audience targeting, and also design the ad creative (images, video, copy) so everything is aligned from day one.

metaMarketing.q: How do you know which ads are actually working?
metaMarketing.a: We set up the Meta Pixel and conversion tracking, then continuously A/B test creative and audiences, doubling down on what performs and cutting what doesn't.

metaMarketing.q: What budget do I need to start?
metaMarketing.a: We can work with a range of budgets — we'll recommend a realistic starting budget based on your goals and industry during a short discovery call.

metaMarketing.q: Will I get clear reports on results, not just impressions?
metaMarketing.a: Yes — we report on metrics that matter to your business: cost per lead/purchase, ROAS, and conversion trends, not just vanity metrics like reach.

metaMarketing.q: How long before we see results?
metaMarketing.a: Initial data comes in within the first 1–2 weeks; most campaigns reach stable, optimized performance after about 4 weeks of testing.

seo.q: Do you start with a technical audit?
seo.a: Yes — we start by auditing site speed, indexing, mobile-friendliness, and structure, and fix technical issues that are holding your rankings back before anything else.

seo.q: How do you choose which keywords to target?
seo.a: We research keywords based on your industry, competition, and search intent, prioritizing terms that bring in visitors who are ready to convert, not just traffic.

seo.q: Do you write the content, or just tell us what to write?
seo.a: Both options are available — we can write SEO-optimized content ourselves, or provide a detailed content brief for your team to execute.

seo.q: Is link building part of the service?
seo.a: Yes — we build authority through ethical, sustainable link-building strategies (no spammy tactics that risk a Google penalty).

seo.q: How long until we see ranking improvements?
seo.a: SEO is a medium-term investment — most sites see meaningful ranking movement within 3–6 months, with technical fixes often showing impact sooner.

jsmPortal.q: What is a JSM Customer Portal, exactly?
jsmPortal.a: It's the self-service front-end for Jira Service Management — a branded portal where customers or employees submit requests, track status, and find answers, without needing Jira access themselves.

jsmPortal.q: Can we have different portals for different teams (IT, HR, facilities)?
jsmPortal.a: Yes — we set up separate request types, queues, and permissions per team, so each department gets its own workflow inside a single JSM instance.

jsmPortal.q: Do you set up SLAs so requests don't fall through the cracks?
jsmPortal.a: Yes. We configure SLA policies per request type and priority, with automatic escalation and reporting so nothing sits unanswered.

jsmPortal.q: Can the portal deflect simple requests with a knowledge base?
jsmPortal.a: Yes — we link a searchable knowledge base to the portal, so common questions get answered instantly instead of becoming tickets.

jsmPortal.q: How long does a JSM Portal setup take?
jsmPortal.a: A standard setup — portal branding, request types, SLAs, and knowledge base — typically takes 1–3 weeks depending on the number of teams involved.

clickup.q: Do you set up ClickUp from scratch, or fix an existing messy workspace?
clickup.a: Both. We can build a clean ClickUp structure from zero, or audit and reorganize an existing workspace that's grown disorganized over time.

clickup.q: Can you connect ClickUp to our other tools?
clickup.a: Yes — we set up integrations with Slack, GitHub, Google Drive, and other tools via native integrations or Zapier, so updates flow automatically between systems.

clickup.q: Will our team need training to use the new setup?
clickup.a: We include onboarding and training as part of every setup, so your team knows exactly how to use the new Spaces, workflows, and automations from day one.

clickup.q: Can you automate repetitive tasks in ClickUp?
clickup.a: Yes — we set up automations for status changes, task assignments, due-date reminders, and recurring tasks, so your team spends less time on manual admin work.

clickup.q: How long does a ClickUp setup take?
clickup.a: A typical workspace setup takes 1–2 weeks, depending on the number of teams, projects, and integrations involved.

support.q: What's included in an SLA support plan?
support.a: A guaranteed response time based on severity (critical, high, normal), 24/7 monitoring, bug fixes, and a direct support channel — so problems get resolved before they affect your users.

support.q: How fast do you respond to critical issues?
support.a: Critical incidents (site down, data loss risk) get a response within the timeframe defined in your SLA — typically under 1 hour, with resolution prioritized over all other work.

support.q: Do you only fix bugs, or also add new features?
support.a: Both. Support plans cover bug fixes and monitoring by default, and we can also allocate ongoing hours for new features and product improvements.

support.q: Can you support an application you didn't originally build?
support.a: Yes. We start with a short codebase and infrastructure audit to understand the system, then take over support and development from there.

support.q: How do we report issues or request changes?
support.a: Through a dedicated channel — usually Slack or Jira Service Management — so every request is tracked, prioritized, and visible to your team.

analytics.q: Do you set up Google Analytics 4 (GA4) from scratch?
analytics.a: Yes — we configure GA4 (or migrate you from Universal Analytics), set up conversion goals, and connect it to Google Tag Manager for clean, reliable event tracking.

analytics.q: Can you track specific actions, like form submissions or button clicks?
analytics.a: Yes. We set up custom event tracking for exactly the actions that matter to your business — signups, purchases, downloads, video plays, and more.

analytics.q: Will I get regular reports, or do I have to check dashboards myself?
analytics.a: Both. We build a live dashboard you can check anytime, and also send periodic reports summarizing traffic, conversions, and trends in plain language.

analytics.q: Do you help make sense of the data, or just set up tracking?
analytics.a: Both — we don't just install tracking, we help interpret what the data means and recommend concrete changes to improve conversion and user experience.
Yes — we configure consent-aware tracking and cookie banners so data collection respects user privacy regulations.

infra.q: Which cloud provider do you work with — AWS, GCP, or Azure?
infra.a: All three. We choose the provider that fits your budget and existing setup, or work within the one you already use — no need to migrate just to work with us.

infra.q: Do you also manage on-premise or self-hosted servers?
infra.a: Yes. Alongside cloud environments, we administer on-prem and self-hosted (VPS, dedicated servers) infrastructure — including Linux administration and Nginx/IIS configuration.

infra.q: How do you handle security and backups?
infra.a: We harden server access, set up firewalls and least-privilege policies, and configure automated daily backups with tested restore procedures — so a failure never means data loss.

infra.q: Can you help reduce our cloud costs?
infra.a: Yes — we audit your current usage and right-size resources, set up autoscaling, and eliminate idle or over-provisioned services. Most clients see a meaningful reduction in their monthly bill.

infra.q: Do you provide ongoing infrastructure support after setup?
infra.a: Yes — we offer ongoing monitoring, maintenance, and on-call support, so your infrastructure keeps running reliably as your traffic and team grow.

devops.q: Do you set up CI/CD from scratch or work with our existing pipeline?
devops.a: Both. We can build a CI/CD pipeline from zero, or audit and improve an existing GitLab/GitHub Actions setup — tightening build times, test coverage, and deployment safety.
No. We set up zero-downtime deployment strategies (rolling updates, blue-green, or canary releases) so your users never see an outage during a release.

devops.q: What happens if a deployment fails?
devops.a: Automatic rollback. If a health check or test fails after deploy, the pipeline reverts to the last stable version without anyone needing to intervene manually.

devops.q: Do you handle monitoring and alerting too?
devops.a: Yes — we set up monitoring (Datadog, Grafana, or similar) with alerts routed to Slack or email, so your team knows about issues before your users do.

devops.q: How long does it take to set up a full CI/CD pipeline?
devops.a: A standard pipeline — build, test, deploy, monitoring — is typically ready in 2–3 weeks, depending on your infrastructure and number of environments.

web.q: Do you build with React or WordPress?
web.a: Both — the right fit depends on your project. React/Next.js suits complex, custom web apps and portals; WordPress suits marketing sites, blogs, and content-managed pages that your team wants to update independently.

web.q: How do I know which one I need?
web.a: If you need custom logic, dashboards, or heavy integrations, React is the better fit. If you mainly need a content-driven site your team can edit without a developer, WordPress is faster and more cost-effective.

web.q: Can I start on WordPress and move to React later?
web.a: Yes. Many clients start with a WordPress site and migrate to a custom React application once their product or traffic outgrows it. We plan the architecture with that path in mind from day one.
Yes — WooCommerce on WordPress for most stores, or a custom React storefront when you need more control over checkout, integrations, or performance at scale.

web.q: How long does a website take to build?
web.a: A WordPress site typically launches in 2–4 weeks. A custom React application usually takes 6–12 weeks, depending on scope and integrations.

mobile.q: Do you build fully native apps, or use Flutter?
mobile.a: We build with Flutter — a single codebase that compiles to fully native iOS and Android apps, so you get native performance and platform UI without paying for two separate teams.

mobile.q: How long does it take to build a mobile app?
mobile.a: Most MVP-scope apps launch in 6–10 weeks depending on features and integrations. We'll confirm a concrete timeline after a short discovery call.

mobile.q: Will the app feel the same on iOS and Android?
mobile.a: Yes. Flutter renders its own UI engine, so your app looks and behaves consistently — and natively — on both platforms from one codebase.

mobile.q: Do you handle App Store and Google Play publishing?
mobile.a: Yes — we manage the entire submission process: developer accounts, store listings, screenshots, and review requirements for both stores.

mobile.q: What happens after the app launches?
mobile.a: We offer ongoing support: bug fixes, OS-version updates, analytics monitoring, and new features as your product grows.
